subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Analysis of Functions
stimulus: Let $f$ be a function that is continuous on the interval $[0, 4)$. The function $f$ is twice differentiable except at $x = 2$. The function $f$ and its derivatives have the properties indicated in the table above, where DNE indicates that the derivatives of $f$ do not exist at $x = 2$.

parts.prompt: For $0 < x < 4$, find all values of $x$ at which $f$ has a relative extremum. Determine whether $f$ has a relative maximum or a relative minimum at each of these values. Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: For $0 < x < 4$, find all values of $x$ at which $f$ has a relative extremum. Determine whether $f$ has a relative maximum or a relative minimum at each of these values. Justify your answer.

parts.prompt: On the axes provided, sketch the graph of a function that has all the characteristics of $f$. (Note: Use the axes provided in the pink test booklet.)
rubric_criteria: On the axes provided, sketch the graph of a function that has all the characteristics of $f$.
(Note: Use the axes provided in the pink test booklet.)

parts.prompt: Let $g$ be the function defined by $g(x) = \int_1^x f(t)\, dt$ on the open interval $(0, 4)$. For $0 < x < 4$, find all values of $x$ at which $g$ has a relative extremum. Determine whether $g$ has a relative maximum or a relative minimum at each of these values. Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: Let $g$ be the function defined by $g(x) = \int_1^x f(t)\, dt$ on the open interval $(0, 4)$. For $0 < x < 4$, find all values of $x$ at which $g$ has a relative extremum. Determine whether $g$ has a relative maximum or a relative minimum at each of these values. Justify your answer.

parts.prompt: For the function $g$ defined in part (c), find all values of $x$, for $0 < x < 4$, at which the graph of $g$ has a point of inflection. Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: For the function $g$ defined in part (c), find all values of $x$, for $0 < x < 4$, at which the graph of $g$ has a point of inflection. Justify your answer.
